summary: Ukraine's Air Force lost a MiG-29 over Poltava Oblast on June 27, 2026, though the pilot ejected safely and was hospitalized. Russian forces continued offensive operations across multiple eastern sectors including Kharkiv, Donetsk, Zaporizhzhia, and Dnipropetrovsk regions, while Ukrainian forces struck infrastructure targets in Russian-held territory and Russia. Ukrainian officials warned that Russia is preparing a large-scale missile and drone attack on Kyiv following Tu-95MS bomber redeployments from Ukrainka airfield.
key_points: A Ukrainian MiG-29 was lost over Poltava Oblast on the night of June 27, 2026; the pilot ejected safely and was taken to a medical facility. (Source: @kpszsu/66567)

Ukraine's General Staff reported multiple Shahed drone attacks targeting Chernihiv (15 drones, Desnianskyi district), Chernihiv TPP (5 drones), and Darnytskyi district of Kyiv (1 reactive drone). (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68088, @mon1tor_ua/68091, @mon1tor_ua/68098)

Ukrainian forces struck the 'Titan-Barrikady' plant in Volgograd with FP-5 'Flamingo' missiles, causing a fire at the facility that produces artillery systems and military equipment. (Source: @UkraineNow/82221)

Russian forces captured the village of Novoselyvate in Dnipropetrovsk Oblast and continue offensive operations in Kharkiv direction (Kazacha Lopan, Vovchansk, Velykyi Burluk sectors) and Zaporizhzhia direction (west of Vozdvyzhenka, Verkhnya Tersa, Komsomolske). (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31209, @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)

Ukrainian officials warned Russia is preparing a large-scale attack on Kyiv using ballistic, cruise, hypersonic missiles and strike drones, to follow after Tu-95MS bombers redeploy from Ukrainka airfield. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68111)
Russian MoD claimed air defense shot down 124 Ukrainian drones over Russian regions and Black Sea waters in one day. (Source: @tass_agency/382692)

Ukrainian drones targeted Russian gas stations in Zaporizhzhia (2 stations hit by Geraniums) and Nikopol on Dnipropetrovsk Oblast, while Russian forces intensified strikes on locomotives using remotely controlled Geranium drones. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204, @tass_agency/382684, @eRadarrua/98379)
Ukraine's HUR MO launched a fundraising campaign for 3 long-range FPV fiber-optic drones capable of destroying Russian equipment en route to Crimea or the front line. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68099)

sections.executive_summary: Ukraine's Air Force lost a MiG-29 over Poltava Oblast on June 27, 2026, though the pilot ejected safely and was hospitalized. Russian forces continued offensive operations across multiple eastern sectors including Kharkiv, Donetsk, Zaporizhzhia, and Dnipropetrovsk regions, while Ukrainian forces struck infrastructure targets in Russian-held territory and Russia. Ukrainian officials warned that Russia is preparing a large-scale missile and drone attack on Kyiv following Tu-95MS bomber redeployments from Ukrainka airfield.

points: A Ukrainian MiG-29 of the Air Force of the Armed Forces of Ukraine was lost during a combat mission over Poltava Oblast on the night of June 27, 2026; the pilot successfully ejected, contacted a search-and-rescue team, and was delivered to a medical facility for examination. (Source: @kpszsu/66567)

Ukrainian forces used FP-5 'Flamingo' missiles to strike the 'Titan-Barrikady' industrial complex in Volgograd, Russia, a facility producing artillery systems and military equipment including missile launcher elements; hits were recorded with subsequent fire on the factory territory. (Source: @UkraineNow/82221)

Ukraine's General Staff reported multiple Shahed drone attacks across northern and eastern Ukraine: 15 drones targeted Chernihiv's Desnianskyi district, 5 drones targeted Chernihiv TPP, 1 reactive drone targeted Darnytskyi district of Kyiv, and groups of drones were tracked moving from Sumy Oblast toward Poltava and from Luhansk Oblast toward Kharkiv. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68088, @mon1tor_ua/68091, @mon1tor_ua/68098, @kpszsu/66544, @kpszsu/66537)

Ukrainian HUR MO launched an urgent fundraising campaign for 3 long-range FPV fiber-optic drones capable of destroying Russian equipment traveling toward Crimea or the front line, with the goal of destroying vehicles before they reach the front. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68099)

Ukrainian drones struck a gas station in Nikopol, Dnipropetrovsk Oblast, while other UAVs were tracked heading toward Dnipro, Kamianske, and Samara/Dnipropetrovsk Oblast areas. (Source: @eRadarrua/98379, @eRadarrua/98382, @kpszsu/66544)

Ukrainian officials warned that Russia is preparing its largest attack on Kyiv involving ballistic, cruise, hypersonic missiles and strike drones, to be launched after Tu-95MS bombers complete redeployment from Ukrainka airfield. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68111)

Ukrainian sources reported that Russian forces are experiencing significant fuel shortages in border regions, with residents queuing overnight at gas stations and unable to refuel despite long waits, attributed partly to Ukrainian strikes on Russian energy infrastructure. (Source: @UkraineNow/82220)

Russian forces captured the village of Novoselyvate in Dnipropetrovsk Oblast, according to the Russian MoD, and the Army Group 'East' captured strongholds on the Velykomihailovka–Alexandrovka line, repelling a counterattack involving three armored combat vehicles. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31209, @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)

Russian forces continue offensive operations on the Kharkiv direction in Kazacha Lopan, Vovchansk sector (Losevka, Ukrainske, Zemlyanoy Yar), and Velykyi Burluk sector (Petro-Ivanovka), and south of Kupyansk in the Kovsharovka area. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)

Russian forces are storming Krasny Liman, striking crossings over the Oskol River, and continuing offensive on the Slavyansk direction from Ray-Aleksandrovka, while the storming of Konstantinovka continues with FAB strikes shifted to the near rear of AFU in Druzhkovka. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)

Russian MoD claimed air defense shot down 124 Ukrainian drones over Russian regions and Black Sea waters in one day, and reported 175 drones destroyed between 7:00 AM and 8:00 PM from June 26. (Source: @tass_agency/382692, @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)

Russian forces used remotely controlled Geranium drones to destroy locomotives — 2 under a bridge in Zaporizhzhia and 3 in Kharkov region — and struck two gas stations in Zaporizhzhia. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204, @tass_agency/382684)

Four Tu-95MS aircraft and two Il-78 tankers conducted training flights over the Sea of Japan without redeployment to airfields closer to Ukraine, as of the reporting period. (Source: @eRadarrua/98356)

Russian forces intensified strikes on Zaporizhzhia city using FAB bombs, and recorded 18 strikes on settlements in Zaporizhzhia region over 24 hours resulting in 1 killed and 4 injured. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)

Russian Army Group 'North' continues offensive operations on the Kharkiv direction in Kazacha Lopan and adjacent forest areas, with enemy counterattacks repelled and prisoners taken. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)

On the Vovchansk sector, small-arms fighting continues in Losevka, Ukrainske, and Zemlyanoy Yar; the enemy made an unsuccessful counterattack attempt with several battle groups in Zemlyanoy Yar. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)
Russian forces are fighting south of Kupyansk in the Kovsharovka area, Kharkov region, and continue storming Krasny Liman while striking crossings over the Oskol River. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)

On the Slavyansk direction, Russian forces operate from Ray-Aleksandrovka on a broad front; the storming of Konstantinovka continues with Russian forces building success in urban areas. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)
Russian forces shifted FAB strikes to the near rear of AFU in Druzhkovka from Konstantinovka direction. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)

In Dnipropetrovsk Oblast, Army Group 'East' captured strongholds on the Velykomihailovka–Alexandrovka line and repelled a counterattack with three armored combat vehicles against Trans-Baikal troops. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)

In eastern Zaporizhzhia region, Russian forces continued wedging into enemy defense west of Vozdvyzhenka, Verkhnya Tersa, and Komsomolske, forcing the enemy to commit reserves on multiple sectors. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)

On the Sumy direction, Army Group 'North' assault troops continue small-arms battles in Bachevsk and surroundings (Shostka district), and in Pysarevka and Novaya Sech (Sumy district). (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)

Multiple Shahed drone groups were tracked from Belgorod Oblast toward southern Sumy Oblast (direction of Okhtyrka), from Chernihiv Oblast toward Slavutych, Horodnia, and Shostka/Novhorod-Siverskyi. (Source: @kpszsu/66537, @kpszsu/66586, @kpszsu/66562, @eRadarrua/98432, @eRadarrua/98398)

15 Shahed drones targeted Chernihiv's Desnianskyi district and 5 drones targeted Chernihiv TPP; air raid alert for Kyiv was lifted at 22:22 on June 27. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68088, @mon1tor_ua/68091, @eRadarrua/98418)
In Kursk region, a Ukrainian UAV attacked a grocery store in Belaya (Belovsky district) wounding a woman; another person was injured in Giri, Belovsky district. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)

In Belgorod region, a Ukrainian FPV drone detonated at an enterprise in Shebekino injuring a civilian; a 15-year-old boy was injured from an FPV drone detonation in Nikolsky khutor; a man was killed from a munition detonation in Ploskoye, Korochansky district. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)
Russian authorities in Bryansk Oblast reported 2 people killed from a Ukrainian drone strike on a parked car near a shop in Petryaevka village. (Source: @tass_agency/382683)

In Zaporizhzhia region, Russian forces recorded 18 strikes on settlements over 24 hours resulting in 1 person killed and 4 injured; AFU continued strikes on infrastructure of the Zaporizhzhia NPP. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)

Russian forces used Geranium drones to destroy 2 locomotives under a bridge in Zaporizhzhia and 3 in Kharkov region, and struck 2 gas stations in Zaporizhzhia. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204, @tass_agency/382684)
In Kherson region, a boy born in 2010 was injured in Sofiyevka (Kakhovsky district); a man and woman were wounded in Kakhovka; a civilian was wounded in Dniprovany. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)
An enemy drone deliberately struck an ambulance in Kakhovsky district, killing a female paramedic and seriously injuring the driver. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)

Ukrainian FPV drones struck an enterprise in Belgorod region's Shebekino, and a managed UAV was tracked from southern Mykolaiv Oblast toward Volodymyrka. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204, @eRadarrua/98380)

Russian forces in Zaporizhzhia direction are being forced to commit reserves on multiple sectors simultaneously due to Russian advances west of Vozdvyzhenka, Verkhnya Tersa, and Komsomolske, limiting AFU reinforcement capacity on Velykomihailovka direction. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)
A Ukrainian MiG-29 was lost over Poltava Oblast on June 27, 2026 night; the pilot ejected safely, contacted rescue services, and was hospitalized. (Source: @kpszsu/66567)

Ukrainian FP-5 'Flamingo' missiles struck the 'Titan-Barrikady' plant in Volgograd's Krasnooktyabrsky district, causing a fire; 10 injured were reported. (Source: @UkraineNow/82221, @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)

Russian MoD claimed 124 Ukrainian drones shot down over Russian regions and Black Sea in one day; 175 drones were reported destroyed between 7:00 AM and 8:00 PM on June 26. (Source: @tass_agency/382692, @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)

Ukrainian officials warned Russia is preparing a large-scale attack on Kyiv with ballistic, cruise, hypersonic missiles and strike drones after Tu-95MS bombers redeploy from Ukrainka airfield. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68111)
Four Tu-95MS and two Il-78 tankers conducted training over the Sea of Japan without redeployment toward Ukraine as of the reporting period. (Source: @eRadarrua/98356)

Russian forces intensified strikes on locomotives using remotely controlled Geranium drones and continued high-intensity strikes including FABs against Zaporizhzhia city. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)

Multiple drone groups were tracked across northern and eastern Ukraine including reactive Shaheds from Sumy toward Poltava, from Luhansk toward Kharkiv (Chuhuyiv direction), and from Poltava toward Dnipropetrovsk Oblast (Mahdalynivka direction). (Source: @kpszsu/66559, @kpszsu/66546, @kpszsu/66537, @kpszsu/66539)

key_developments: Ukraine lost a MiG-29 over Poltava Oblast on June 27, 2026 night; pilot ejected safely and was hospitalized; circumstances under investigation. (Source: @kpszsu/66567)

Ukrainian FP-5 'Flamingo' missiles struck the Titan-Barrikady plant in Volgograd producing artillery systems and military equipment, causing a fire and 10 injuries. (Source: @UkraineNow/82221, @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)

Ukrainian officials warned Russia is preparing a large-scale attack on Kyiv with ballistic, cruise, hypersonic missiles and strike drones after Tu-95MS redeployment from Ukrainka airfield. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68111)

Russian forces captured Novoselyvate in Dnipropetrovsk Oblast and advanced on Velykomihailovka–Alexandrovka line, repelling a counterattack with three armored vehicles. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31209, @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)

Russian forces continue multi-axis offensive operations: Kharkiv direction (Kazacha Lopan, Vovchansk, Velykyi Burluk), Slavyansk direction, Konstantinovka, and Krasny Liman. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)

15 Shahed drones targeted Chernihiv's Desnianskyi district and 5 targeted Chernihiv TPP; Russian MoD claimed 124 Ukrainian drones shot down in one day. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68088, @mon1tor_ua/68091, @tass_agency/382692)

Russian forces struck 2 locomotives under a bridge in Zaporizhzhia and 3 in Kharkov region using Geranium drones, and hit 2 gas stations in Zaporizhzhia. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204, @tass_agency/382684)
sections.threat_assessment: The conflict is entering an escalatory phase with both sides intensifying strikes on strategic infrastructure. Russia is preparing a large-scale combined arms attack on Kyiv involving ballistic, cruise, hypersonic missiles and strike drones, pending Tu-95MS redeployment from Ukrainka airfield. Simultaneously, Ukraine's successful strike on the Titan-Barrikady plant in Volgograd demonstrates deep-strike capability against Russian defense industrial targets, likely prompting further Russian retaliation. The sustained drone campaign against Russian energy infrastructure is reportedly causing fuel shortages in Russian border regions, creating operational and logistical pressure on Russian forces.
sections.regional_response: Poland closed the Russian consulate general in Gdańsk, with Russian Foreign Ministry spokesperson Zakharova characterizing this as demonstrating 'who the real aggressor is.' China and Russia conducted joint air patrols over the Sea of Japan, East China Sea, and western Pacific with nearly 10 aircraft, entering South Korea's air defense identification zone and prompting Seoul to scramble fighters. The US struck Iran for the first time since a ceasefire agreement, with each side accusing the other of violating the truce terms. Putin and Lukashenko met at the Russian president's Valdai residence to discuss Union State agenda, trade-economic cooperation, and regional security.
sections.intelligence_notes: The loss of the MiG-29 over Poltava Oblast is a notable degradation of Ukraine's air combat capability, though pilot survival preserves experienced personnel. Russian offensive activity across Kharkiv, Donetsk, Zaporizhzhia, and Dnipropetrovsk directions indicates continued multi-axis pressure despite reported fuel logistics challenges. The Ukrainian HUR MO's fundraising for 3 long-range FPV fiber-optic drones signals prioritization of deep-strike anti-armor capability targeting Russian logistics routes to Crimea and the front. Russian claims of 124 drones downed in one day and 175 over a longer period suggest sustained Ukrainian drone operations at scale, though actual Ukrainian sortie rates and Russian interception rates require independent verification.
